CheckpointConfig.description: Checkpoint configuration for pipeline resume capability.

ClusterConfig.description:
Table clustering configuration.

Destination-Specific Behavior:
    - Databricks: Uses Liquid Clustering (not ZORDER). Use "AUTO" for automatic.
                 Cannot use with partition on same table.
    - BigQuery: Uses cluster_by, max 4 fields enforced by BigQuery.
    - Oracle/MSSQL: Not supported (validation will reject).

Note: Field count validation is done at runtime based on destination type.

fields.description: Fields to cluster by, or 'AUTO' for Databricks auto liquid clustering
fields.title: Fields

ClusterConfig.title: ClusterConfig

DatabaseDestinationConfig.description:
Database/DWH destination configuration.

Supports Databricks Unity Catalog and BigQuery.
Reference: https://dlthub.com/docs/dlt-ecosystem/destinations/databricks

catalog.description: Unity Catalog name (Databricks) or project ID (BigQuery)
catalog.title: Catalog

DatabaseDestinationConfig.title: DatabaseDestinationConfig

DateGranularity.description:
Date granularity for folder partitioning.

Based on GRM Germany FileRepositoryDateGranularity pattern.
DateGranularity.title: DateGranularity

DuckDBDestinationConfig.description:
DuckDB destination configuration for local testing.

DuckDB is an in-process OLAP database perfect for local development
and CI/CD testing without requiring cloud credentials.

Usage:
    # In-memory database (ephemeral, lost after process ends)
    destination:
      type: "duckdb"
      database: ":memory:"
      schema: "main"
    
    # Persistent file (survives process restart)
    destination:
      type: "duckdb"
      database: "test_output.duckdb"
      schema: "test_data"

Reference: https://dlthub.com/docs/dlt-ecosystem/destinations/duckdb

database.description: DuckDB file path or ':memory:' for in-memory database
database.title: Database

DuckDBDestinationConfig.title: DuckDBDestinationConfig

FileCompression.description: Supported compression types for file output.
FileCompression.title: FileCompression

FileFormat.description:
Supported file formats for filesystem source/destination.

dlt NATIVE READERS:
    - CSV:     read_csv() via pandas, or read_csv_duckdb() for better performance
    - PARQUET: read_parquet() via pyarrow
    - JSONL:   read_jsonl() - line-delimited JSON

FRAMEWORK-PROVIDED TRANSFORMERS:
    - JSON:  Single JSON files - framework provides read_json() transformer

REQUIRES CUSTOM TRANSFORMERS (contact central team):
    - AVRO:  Apache Avro format (requires fastavro)
    - XLSX:  Excel files (requires openpyxl)

PERFORMANCE NOTES:
    - Use read_csv_duckdb() instead of read_csv() for better CSV performance
    - Set use_pyarrow=True for Parquet/CSV to enable zero-copy Arrow format
    - Increase chunk_size for large files (10,000+ recommended for production)
FileFormat.title: FileFormat

FilesystemConnectionConfig.description:
Filesystem connection configuration.

Supports multiple protocols: local, Azure ADLS, S3, GCS, SFTP.

account_name.description: Storage account name (Azure) or access key ID (AWS)
account_name.title: Account Name

protocol.description: Filesystem protocol (az, s3, gs, file, sftp)

FilesystemConnectionConfig.title: FilesystemConnectionConfig

FilesystemDestinationConfig.description:
Filesystem destination configuration for writing to partitioned folders.

Use cases:
    - Data lake staging before DWH load
    - Cross-system data exchange
    - Backup/archive to cloud storage

Partition/Cluster Mapping:
    - partition.field → Folder structure based on date_granularity
    - cluster.fields → File-level row sorting within partitions

cluster.description: Cluster config (fields map to file-level sorting)

partition.description: Partition config (field maps to folder structure)

FilesystemDestinationConfig.title: FilesystemDestinationConfig

FilesystemFileOptionsConfig.description:
File format, compression, and performance options for filesystem operations.

Note: file_glob moved to FilesystemPathConfig for source reading.
This class handles format, compression, performance tuning, and output file naming.

PERFORMANCE TUNING:
    - chunk_size: Number of rows/records per batch. Larger = faster but more memory.
    - use_pyarrow: Enable zero-copy Arrow format for Parquet/CSV (faster for large files).
    - use_duckdb_csv_reader: Use DuckDB instead of pandas for CSV (faster).

RECOMMENDED SETTINGS BY FILE SIZE:
    - Small files (<10MB): chunk_size=1000, defaults are fine
    - Medium files (10-100MB): chunk_size=10000
    - Large files (>100MB): chunk_size=50000, use_pyarrow=True

chunk_size.description:
Number of rows/records to process per batch.
Larger values = better throughput but higher memory usage.
Recommended: 1000 (small files), 10000 (medium), 50000+ (large)
chunk_size.title: Chunk Size

compression.description: Compression type (snappy, gzip, lz4, zstd, none)

file_name_pattern.title: File Name Pattern

format.description: File format (parquet, avro, json, jsonl, csv)

max_file_size_mb.description: Maximum output file size in MB before splitting
max_file_size_mb.title: Max File Size Mb

use_duckdb_csv_reader.description:
Use DuckDB reader for CSV files instead of pandas.
DuckDB is typically faster and handles compression better.
Set to False only if you need specific pandas CSV options.
use_duckdb_csv_reader.title: Use Duckdb Csv Reader

use_pyarrow.description:
Use PyArrow RecordBatch format instead of Python dicts.
Enables zero-copy operations for better performance with large files.
Only effective for Parquet and CSV (with DuckDB reader).
Set to True when loading large files (>100MB) to Databricks.
use_pyarrow.title: Use Pyarrow

FilesystemFileOptionsConfig.title: FilesystemFileOptionsConfig

FilesystemPathConfig.description:
Filesystem path configuration with flexible partitioning support.

Supports TWO patterns:

1. MULTI-FOLDER PARTITIONED (GRM Germany style):
   Base: file-repository/Trading/Oracle
   Folders: {year}/{month}/{day}/ or year={year}/month={month}/day={day}/
   Files: *.parquet
   Result: file-repository/Trading/Oracle/2024/01/28/*.parquet

2. SINGLE-FOLDER with date-in-filename:
   Base: landing-zone/partner-feed
   Folders: null (no folder partitioning)
   Files: data_{year}{month}{day}*.csv
   Result: landing-zone/partner-feed/data_20240128*.csv

MULTI-LEVEL FOLDER STRUCTURES:
   The base_path handles any static folder hierarchy BEFORE date folders:
   
   Example 1: germany/hr/advisor/2026/01/01/
     base_path: "germany/hr/advisor"
     folder_pattern: "{year}/{month}/{day}/"
   
   Example 2: uk/2026/01/01/
     base_path: "uk"
     folder_pattern: "{year}/{month}/{day}/"
   
   Example 3: data/region=emea/country=de/2026/01/01/
     base_path: "data/region=emea/country=de"
     folder_pattern: "{year}/{month}/{day}/"

Path pattern variables:
    - {year}, {month}, {day}, {hour}: Date components
    - {source_name}, {table_name}: Runtime substitution
    - {timestamp}, {load_id}: Load-time values

date_granularity.description: Date granularity for folder partitioning or filename date extraction

file_glob.description:
Glob pattern for matching files. Supports date variables for single-folder pattern.
Examples: '*.parquet', 'data_{year}{month}{day}*.csv', '**/*.json'
file_glob.title: File Glob

folder_pattern.description:
Folder partition pattern with date variables. Set to null for single-folder sources where date is in filename.
Examples: '{year}/{month}/{day}/', 'year={year}/month={month}/day={day}/' (Hive-style)
folder_pattern.title: Folder Pattern

partition_style.description: Partition folder naming style (numeric or hive_style)

FilesystemPathConfig.title: FilesystemPathConfig

include_error_details.description: Include error details in failure notifications
include_error_details.title: Include Error Details

PerformanceConfig.description:
Performance tuning configuration for dlt pipeline stages.

These settings map to dlt environment variables for parallelization
and buffer management. Apply these BEFORE creating pipelines or sources.

Reference: https://dlthub.com/docs/reference/performance

Stage Worker Types:
    - EXTRACT: Thread pool (I/O bound) - fetching data from sources
    - NORMALIZE: Process pool (CPU bound) - converting data to load format
    - LOAD: Thread pool (I/O bound) - writing to destination

File Rotation:
    Enable file rotation to unlock parallel processing. Without rotation,
    1 resource = 1 file = 1 worker. With rotation, 1 resource = many files
    = many workers can process in parallel.

Example YAML:
    performance:
      extract_workers: 5
      normalize_workers: 4
      load_workers: 20
      extract_file_max_items: 100000
      normalize_file_max_items: 100000

Environment Variables Set:
    - EXTRACT__WORKERS
    - NORMALIZE__WORKERS  
    - LOAD__WORKERS
    - EXTRACT__DATA_WRITER__BUFFER_MAX_ITEMS
    - NORMALIZE__DATA_WRITER__BUFFER_MAX_ITEMS
    - EXTRACT__DATA_WRITER__FILE_MAX_ITEMS
    - NORMALIZE__DATA_WRITER__FILE_MAX_ITEMS
    - NORMALIZE__DATA_WRITER__DISABLE_COMPRESSION

add_dlt_id.description: Add _dlt_id column with unique row identifier. Incurs performance overhead as files must be rewritten. Not typically needed for Bronze append-only patterns.
add_dlt_id.title: Add Dlt Id

add_dlt_load_id.description: Add _dlt_load_id column to all tables. This column tracks which load operation created each row. Essential for Bronze layer auditing. Default True because dlt's Arrow/pyarrow backend skips this by default.
add_dlt_load_id.title: Add Dlt Load Id

disable_normalize_compression.description: Disable compression during normalization for speed (debugging only).
disable_normalize_compression.title: Disable Normalize Compression

enable_monitoring.description: Enable performance monitoring to verify worker pool configuration. When enabled, captures actual worker counts via monkey-patching dlt internals. Minimal overhead (<1%). Opt-in for validation and debugging only.
enable_monitoring.title: Enable Monitoring

extract_buffer_max_items.description: Items in extract buffer before writing to file.
extract_buffer_max_items.title: Extract Buffer Max Items

extract_file_max_items.description: Items per extract file (enables file rotation). Set to 100000 for large extractions to enable parallel normalize.
extract_file_max_items.title: Extract File Max Items

extract_workers.description: Thread count for parallel resource extraction. Increase for multiple independent API endpoints or database tables.
extract_workers.title: Extract Workers

load_workers.description: Thread count for parallel loading (I/O-bound). Default 20 is usually sufficient.
load_workers.title: Load Workers

normalize_buffer_max_items.description: Items in normalize buffer before writing to file.
normalize_buffer_max_items.title: Normalize Buffer Max Items

normalize_file_max_items.description: Items per normalized file (enables file rotation). Set to 100000 for large loads to enable parallel loading.
normalize_file_max_items.title: Normalize File Max Items

normalize_workers.description: Process count for parallel normalization (CPU-bound). Recommended: number of CPU cores - 1.
normalize_workers.title: Normalize Workers

PerformanceConfig.title: PerformanceConfig

fail_pipeline_on_failure.description: Fail pipeline if any DQ check fails
fail_pipeline_on_failure.title: Fail Pipeline On Failure

dev_mode.description: Dev mode resets schema/state on each run (use True for development)
dev_mode.title: Dev Mode

log_level.description: Logging level for pipeline file handler (DEBUG captures HTTP response codes like 429/500)
log_level.title: Log Level

RetryConfig.description:
Configuration for retry behavior with exponential backoff.

Uses tenacity library patterns.
Reference: https://tenacity.readthedocs.io/

retriable_exceptions.description: Exception types that should trigger retry
retriable_exceptions.title: Retriable Exceptions

retriable_status_codes.description: HTTP status codes that should trigger retry (for REST APIs)
retriable_status_codes.title: Retriable Status Codes

RetryConfig.title: RetryConfig

SchemaContract.description:
Schema contract configuration for controlling schema evolution.

Controls three dimensions of schema evolution at the destination:

1. **tables**: Can new tables be created?
   - evolve: Allow new tables (recommended for Bronze, needed for first run)
   - freeze: Block new tables (raises PipelineStepFailed)
   - discard_row: Drop rows for unknown tables
   - discard_value: Not applicable for tables dimension

2. **columns**: Can new columns be added to existing tables?
   - evolve: Allow new columns (flexible, good for development)
   - freeze: Block new columns (strict production governance)
   - discard_row: Drop entire row if new column detected
   - discard_value: Set new column values to NULL, keep row

3. **data_type**: Can column data types change?
   - evolve: Allow type changes (may cause issues)
   - freeze: Block type changes (recommended for production)
   - discard_row: Drop row if type mismatch
   - discard_value: Set mismatched values to NULL

Recommended Defaults:
    - Development: tables=evolve, columns=evolve, data_type=evolve
    - Production: tables=evolve, columns=freeze, data_type=freeze

Why tables=evolve in production?
    - First run: Tables don't exist yet, dlt must create them
    - Child tables: dlt auto-creates nested tables (e.g., orders__items)
    - New tables: When you add a new table to config

Reference: https://dlthub.com/docs/general-usage/schema-contracts

Example YAML:
    schema_contract:
      tables: evolve      # Allow table creation
      columns: freeze     # Block new columns
      data_type: freeze   # Block type changes

columns.description: Control new column addition (evolve, freeze, discard_row, discard_value)

data_type.description: Control column type changes (evolve, freeze, discard_row, discard_value)

tables.description: Control new table creation (evolve, freeze, discard_row, discard_value)

SchemaContract.title: SchemaContract

SchemaEvolutionMode.description:
Schema evolution modes for dlt schema_contract.

Reference: https://dlthub.com/docs/general-usage/schema-contracts
SchemaEvolutionMode.title: SchemaEvolutionMode

SecretsProviderConfig.description:
Configuration for secrets resolution.

Secrets in YAML configs use ${secrets.xxx} pattern.
This configuration determines how those references are resolved at runtime.

key_mapping lets users map YAML secret keys (${secrets.xxx}) to
the actual secret names in their provider. This keeps the framework
agnostic to provider naming conventions (e.g., KV allows only
alphanumeric + dashes, Databricks scopes may use slashes, etc.).
When key_mapping is omitted, the raw YAML key is passed as-is.

Example YAML:
    secrets_provider:
      provider: "azure_keyvault"
      vault_url: "https://my-vault.vault.azure.net/"
      key_mapping:
        azure_sql_host: "azure-sql-host"
        azure_sql_password: "azure-sql-password"

Azure Key Vault requires:
    - azure-identity package
    - azure-keyvault-secrets package
    - Proper RBAC/access policies configured

key_mapping.description: Maps ${secrets.xxx} keys to provider-specific secret names. Left side = YAML key (from ${secrets.xxx}), right side = name in provider. If a key is not in the mapping, it is passed to the provider as-is.
key_mapping.title: Key Mapping

prefix.description: Environment variable prefix (for environment provider, e.g., 'DLT_' -> DLT_SECRET_NAME)
prefix.title: Prefix

provider.description: Secrets provider type (dlt_secrets_file, databricks, azure_keyvault, or environment)

scope.description: Databricks secret scope name (for databricks provider)
scope.title: Scope

vault_url.description: Azure Key Vault URL (for azure_keyvault provider)
vault_url.title: Vault Url

SecretsProviderConfig.title: SecretsProviderConfig

SecretsProviderType.description:
Supported secrets providers.

- DLT_SECRETS_FILE: .dlt/secrets.toml file (dlt native, local development default)
- DATABRICKS: Databricks secret scopes (production on Databricks)
- AZURE_KEYVAULT: Azure Key Vault (Azure environments, managed identity)
- ENVIRONMENT: Environment variables (CI/CD, local development)
SecretsProviderType.title: SecretsProviderType

pipeline.description:
Pipeline configuration.

File location: configs/pipelines/{pipeline_name}.yaml

This is the main entry point for running an ingestion pipeline.

performance.description: Performance tuning configuration for extract/normalize/load stages. Controls worker counts, buffer sizes, and file rotation.

resource_configs.description: Paths to resource configuration files. Optional for REST API sources (resources embedded in source config). Required for database and filesystem sources.
resource_configs.title: Resource Configs

schema_contract.description: Pipeline-level schema contract default. Controls schema evolution at destination. Default: tables=evolve, columns=freeze, data_type=freeze. Individual tables can override via table-level schema_contract.

secrets_provider.description: Secrets provider configuration (databricks, azure_keyvault, or environment)

pipeline.title: PipelineConfig
